title: centaurea maramarosiensis
intro: Centaurea maramarosiensis, also known as Hungarian cornflower, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family, characterized by its blue-violet flowers and distinctive foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Centaurea maramarosiensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support respiratory health. The plant is typically prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture for use in herbal medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
